purpose: Define the external review and conformance path for GDPR and the current ISO AI standards mapping wave inside OpenCompliance.

reviewTracks.goal: Challenge GDPR article-level and obligation-level anchors, including rights and processor boundaries.
Challenge candidate-only ISO AI mappings and the decision to publish or withhold them.
Challenge whether typed claim schemas match the intended GDPR and AI evidence-bearing atoms.
Challenge whether the public site overclaims what is reviewed, proved, or only candidate-level.

reviewerRoles.description: Reviewer who can challenge GDPR rights, processor, transfer, and legal-boundary language.
Reviewer who can challenge whether the evidence and workflow layer really supports privacy operations in practice.
Reviewer who can challenge ISO AI standards inventory, maturity state, and candidate mapping choices.
Reviewer who can challenge whether the current AI corridor is honest about documentary versus decidable coverage.
Formal methods reviewer who can challenge proof-boundary choices when AI or GDPR controls enter Lean.
Implementation reviewer for evidence, fixtures, and conformance behavior.
External scrutiny role to ensure the public site remains honest about claims and limitations.

phases.goal: Ensure current framework inventory, coverage, and publication boundaries are recorded before asking for external review time.
passCriteria: Framework priorities and coverage are current.
Candidate-only and zero-coverage states are explicit.

phases.goal: Challenge the widened GDPR article-level layer and its rights-related and processor-related control boundaries.
passCriteria: Article-level anchors are challengeable and clearly sourced.
Public wording stays honest about proved versus attested privacy controls.

phases.goal: Challenge candidate-only ISO AI mappings before they are widened into the public control surface.
passCriteria: Candidate mappings are either strengthened, blocked, or retired.
Draft-watch frameworks are not presented as stable reviewed layers.

phases.goal: Check that the site and public repos do not overclaim the state of GDPR or AI standards mapping.
passCriteria: The public site keeps reviewed, candidate, and watch-list states explicit.
No page implies certification or legal sufficiency from partial mapping coverage.

remainingBlockers: the wider GDPR corridor still needs more reviewed public-law decomposition beyond the current deployer slice
most ISO AI mappings remain candidate-only or zero-coverage until stronger review material exists
